introduction: An important cause of diarrhoea, caused by pathogenic species of Shigella A-D (dysenteri, flexneri, boydii and sonnei).

drug: Ciprofloxacin 500 mg - 1 g orally 12 hourly for 5 days
Azithromycin 500 mg daily for 3 days for resistant strains
Cotrimoxazole for children: 6 weeks – 6 months: 120 mg every 12 hours, 6 months – 6 years: 240 mg every 12 hours, 6 years – 12 years: 480 mg every 12 hours, 12 years - 18 years: 960 mg every 12 hours
adverse_reactions_and_cautions: Ciprofloxacin causes tendinitis especially in children and so is contraindicated in pregnancy and in children <18 years

prevention: Potable water, sanitary disposal of faeces.
